channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: U.S. Indictment Accuses Mexican Governor of Conspiring With Sinaloa Cartel
item.description: Rubén Rocha Moya, Sinaloa's governor, was accused of conspiring with the Sinaloa Cartel. Prosecutors accused Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of Sinaloa state, and other Mexican officials of a yearslong conspiracy to protect the powerful cartel.

item.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
item.description: King Charles laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial in New York. The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families. King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit.

item.title: Oil price rises above $120 after reports of 'extended' Iran blockade
item.description: Oil prices rose above $120 due to reports of an extended Iran blockade. The price of crude oil has swung sharply as uncertainty over the war in the Middle East continues.

item.title: Hegseth clashes with lawmakers over Iran war in nearly six-hour-long hearing
item.description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth faced lawmakers in a six-hour hearing on the Iran war. US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth sparred with Democratic lawmakers during a nearly six-hour-long hearing, in his first time facing questions under oath since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Florida lawmakers approve new voting maps to favour Republicans
item.description: Florida lawmakers approved new voting maps to favour Republican candidates. The state is the latest to act in a national redistricting war that could shape what political party controls the US House of Representatives.

item.title: The Austrian nuns who fled their care home are now in Rome and visited the Vatican
item.description: Three Austrian nuns visited the Vatican after fleeing their care home. They attended a general audience with Pope Leo XIV at St. Peter's Square. The three octogenarian nuns, who made headlines last year after they broke back into their convent, joined others at St.

item.title: Israel intercepts Global Sumud boats, demands activists halt their mission
item.description: Israel has intercepted seven of 58 vessels from the Global Sumud Flotilla near Crete. The flotilla aimed to deliver aid to Gaza under blockade. Activists were demanded to halt their mission.

item.title: Trump Says US Has "A Shot" At Crewed Moon Landing Before Presidency Ends
item.description: Donald Trump says the US has a chance to land a crewed mission on the Moon before his presidency ends. He welcomed the Artemis II astronauts at the White House.

item.title: Nuclear test ban treaty chief warns of rising global security threats as key states stall on
item.description: The head of the nuclear test ban treaty warned of rising global security threats due to key states stalling on ratification. This comes as tensions escalate globally. The treaty's effectiveness is hindered by major countries not signing on.

item.title: Arsenal cruelly denied penalty by VAR in Champions League draw at Atletico
item.description: Arsenal was denied a penalty by VAR in a Champions League semifinal draw against Atletico Madrid. The match ended 1-1. VAR's decision was controversial. Arsenal scored one penalty, but VAR controversially denied them another in a 1-1 semifinal draw at Atletico Madrid.

item.title: New Florida map boosts Republican seats amid national redistricting fight
item.description: Florida's new map boosts Republican seats ahead of midterm elections. The state is among several redrawing electoral maps. The changes impact the national electoral landscape. Florida has become latest state to redraw map, as national electoral landscape transforms before midterm elections.

item.title: US accuses Mexican governor of working with drug lord's sons
item.description: The US charged the governor of Mexico's Sinaloa state with drug trafficking and weapons offences. Nine other officials were also charged. The US has charged the governor of Mexico's Sinaloa state and nine other current or former officials with drug trafficking and weapons offences.

item.title: Putin offered to help settle Iranian nuclear enrichment impasse
item.description: Vladimir Putin told Donald Trump he'd like to be involved in settling Iran's nuclear enrichment issue, according to Trump. The US has been trying to negotiate a deal with Iran. Details of Putin's offer are unclear.

item.title: Why is the UAE quitting OPEC – and what’s the impact?
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is quitting OPEC, citing the worst global energy crisis in modern times. The move is seen as a significant shift in the global energy landscape. The UAE's decision is effective January 2025.

item.title: Ukraine asks Israel to seize ship carrying grain ‘stolen’ by Russia
item.description: Ukraine has asked Israel to seize a ship carrying grain allegedly stolen by Russia. Kyiv wants Israel to take grain samples and question crew members. The move is part of Ukraine's efforts to recover stolen assets.

item.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran war and float temporary Ukraine ceasefire in call
item.description: US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin discussed a temporary Ukraine ceasefire and the Iran war during a recent phone call. Trump and Putin discuss Iran war and float temporary Ukraine ceasefire in call The Guardian.

item.title: "I Literally Was A Fool": Elon Musk Grilled In OpenAI Trial
item.description: Elon Musk testified in the OpenAI trial. He called for the company to revert to a nonprofit. Elon Musk, who helped co-found OpenAI, has called for it to be forced to revert to a pure nonprofit.

item.title: Seven lawsuits filed against OpenAI by families of Canada mass-shooting victims
item.description: Seven lawsuits were filed against OpenAI by families of Canada mass-shooting victims. The lawsuits accuse OpenAI of negligence. The lawsuits, filed in California, accuse OpenAI and Sam Altman of negligence and abetting a mass shooting by failing to flag the suspect's ChatGPT activity.

item.title: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
item.description: Hungary's next PM Péter Magyar met EU leaders in Brussels. He said frozen EU funds will be paid out soon. Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

item.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
item.description: The US war in Iran has cost an estimated $25 billion over the past two months, according to the Pentagon, but a definitive end date remains unclear. The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months.
